title: Grafana Dashboard Version History and Revert Event Tracking
app: Grafana Enterprise audit or open-source audit file shipping
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit"
| search dashboard AND (action="*save*" OR action="*restore*" OR action="*version*" OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)revert|rollback"))
| stats count, earliest(_time) as first_t, latest(_time) as last_t, values(action) as actions by dashboard_uid, user
| eval actions_join=mvjoin(actions, " ")
| where count >= 5 OR match(lower(actions_join),"(?i)revert|restore")
| sort -count
description: Frequent saves or sudden reverts on production dashboards may indicate edit wars, automation mistakes, or unauthorized changes. A consolidated audit trail supports governance without blocking legitimate iteration.
value: Preserves dashboard integrity for executive KPIs and regulated operational views.
implementation: Map `dashboard_uid` to human titles via nightly API export. Alert on `revert` actions outside business hours. Pair with GitOps provisioning diffs if dashboards are code-managed.
visualization: Timeline (version events), sankey (user → dashboard), count of reverts per org.
grandmaExplanation: We track when important charts are edited or rolled back to older versions. That helps you notice fights over numbers or changes that should not have happened.
